expected_properties: When Q[0] is 0, the next Q has MSB set to 1 and the rest shifted right.
When Q[0] is 1, the next Q has MSB set to 0 and the rest shifted right.
Observed Q transitions follow the Johnson counter rule without X or Z values.
additional_cases.expected_transition: Observe Q transition from a state with LSB=0 to the next state with MSB=1, then from LSB=1 to MSB=0.
additional_cases.goal: Validate the direction‑change behavior of the Johnson counter when the least‑significant bit toggles.
notes: The case does not assume a fixed number of cycles for the transition; it observes the first two clock edges after reset release.
preconditions: Counter has been released from reset and observed for at least two clock cycles.
stimulus_intent: Release reset, allow the counter to run, capture Q before and after the LSB toggles.

stimulus_program.text: One clock cycle while reset is asserted
Observe two clock edges to capture LSB toggle

timing_assumptions: Conservative observation across multiple clock cycles; no fixed latency is assumed.

expected_properties: Q remains defined (no X/Z) after reset release.
Q changes according to the Johnson counter rule on successive clock edges.
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Observe clock‑driven state progress after reset release and verify that Q evolves consistently.
notes: The enrichment adds explicit observation steps while preserving the original conservative intent.
stimulus_intent: Release reset and record Q over a few clock cycles to confirm stable progression.

stimulus_program.text: One clock cycle with reset asserted
Observe three clock edges after reset release

timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations; do not assume a fixed‑cycle completion.
